steps.title: Convert the percent decrease
steps.content:
A $3\%$ decrease means each year keeps $97\%$ of the previous year's enrollment:
$$1-0.03=0.97$$

steps.title: Build the exponential model
steps.content:
The initial enrollment in 2018 is $4{,}200$, and $x$ counts years after 2018. Use initial value times yearly multiplier:
$$f(x)=4{,}200(0.97)^x$$

steps.title: Match the choice
steps.content: Choice D — $f(x) = 4,200 \times (0.97)^x$ is the only function with initial value $4{,}200$ and decay factor $0.97$. Therefore, D is correct.
